caption.text:
Bravo, Director
A "show" "by" Rafael Collado
Have you ever wanted to stop trying?

actions.prompt: Generate a 90-second modern monologue that continues after the introductory sentence 'Have you ever wanted to just stop trying?' Do NOT include that introductory sentence in your output — begin with the very next sentence. Output just the raw text, no preamble, labels, or formatting.

performer.note: AI monologue generating. Read lines as they appear in RED.

caption.text: Don’t you just love AI?
Isn’t it so creative? And fast?
And it sounds so human!
Just the other day, I had a really good therapy session with Gemini.
And it was so right, I should stop working so hard.
Like it said, “It’s not just ‘being lazy’, it’s survival and self-preservation.”
Now that’s what I want to hear!
You know, I really don’t think AI has been wrong about anything ever!
Because, doesn’t it learn basically everything from the internet, which, as we all know, is always true and good information?
AI is to life as ranch is to food;
I just put that shit on everything!
Look, I think you guys are gonna love this next thing I can do.
I'm about to drop the hottest beat and freestyle rap you've ever heard using every single suggestion from the audience.
Take out your phones, scan this QR code, and give me some inspiration for the rap.
I’ll give you all some time.

performer.note: Loads audience words from DynamoDB for the next line.

caption.text: Tonight's suggestions:

performer.note: Read the suggestions aloud, then say the cue to start the beat.

actions.prompt: 87 bpm, boom-bap MF Doom type rap beat, hip hop, groovy, bouncy, grimy, hardcore, chopped and screwed, piano, lots of bass, heavy kick, laid back, chill, ONLY INSTRUMENTAL, NO VOCALS.
Please create a graphic word cloud using all the words in the following list:

Create original rap lyrics that incorporate and reference the following compiled words, phrases, and ideas. Each verse should consist of four couplets, with the number of verses being determined by the number of words, phrases, and ideas in the compiled lyrics. DO NOT write a chorus, bridge, intro, or outro — just write verses. Make sure each line is not too long or short so that it is easy to fit to a mid-tempo beat. The song should creatively weave in themes, phrases, or ideas from these lyrics while being a cohesive, original composition. The tone is boastful and braggadocious rap that is self-referential to the fact that the rapper is the best because he is able to reference these varied ideas in a way that is clever and funny. Output ONLY the raw lyric lines, one per line. No verse labels, no section headers, no brackets, no annotations — just the lyrics.

caption.text: Shoutout to Stability AI for that sick beat!
I do feel bad for all the rappers that are gonna lose their jobs when they see what I can do, though.

trigger.phrase: respect rap as an art form

caption.text: But let me guess, you guys probably don’t respect rap as an art form.
I can see it written all over your faces, I bet you all like to say things like,
“Well actually, you just don't get John Cage like I do!”
"Pauline Oliveros is actually like super underrated."
"Stockhausen was a genius!!1!11!!"
Dude, SCREW Stockhausen, specifically.
Listen to this:
This goes on for far longer than it needs to.
You know, I really liked the part where it went "BEEP BOOP BOP BOOP", really riveting stuff.
I remember listening to that piece in college, and we all had to sit there and say nice things about it.
Well you know what, it sounds STUPID, and Stockhausen SUCKS.
Pauline Oliveros is lowkey chill, though.
Anyway, AI can do the same thing as any "sophisticated" composer, I'll prove it.
Here’s a completely new composition that will impress even the most acclaimed music scholar by how perfect it is.

actions.prompt: Show me the artwork for a piece of music that is an avant-garde post-modern composition for solo vocalist.

Write a modern avant-garde musical piece for solo voice using only plain text output that describes the performance instructions. It should be inspired by very sophisticated composers like Pauline Oliveros, John Cage, Nancarrow, Alvin Lucier, Bach, Mozart, Beethoven, Thor Magnusson, Miller Puckette, Beyonce, Dua Lipa, but without mentioning any of them explicitly by name. The piece should be extremely complex, yet simple and elegant. It should be able to impress a PhD in Music and any other pretentious music nerds. However, as a limitation, the outputted plain text should only be around 100 words. The piece must be perfect and masterful. NO markdown or list formatting. Just plain english sentences.

performer.note: AI voice takes over. Captions driven by TTS timestamps. Stand still.

caption.text: Okay, cool.
What, did you think I was gonna perform that?
Don’t you see, the idea of the composition is just as good as the composition itself!
And with AI, I can do anything, be anyone!
For instance, I love turning myself into an anime character and then posting it on Instagram for everyone to see.
Don't I look so kawaii?
Definitely worth several gallons of fresh water to me!
And I love seeing myself do things I definitely didn’t do, and then making people believe that I did!
I can dunk a basketball, believe me that's a fact
I can solve a Rubik's cube, but wait, behind my back
I can run a mile in 90 seconds flat
And I can pull 10 rabbits out a tiny bowler hat
You might think I’m lying, but if you’ve got eyes to see,
I’ve got the proof on video, so you’ll believe me
I got my PhD when I was only thirteen
And sorry guys, I’m patient zero of COVID-19
I got wrapped inside the Jan. 6th insurrection
And I was in Jerusalem at Jesus’s resurrection
You might think I’m lying, but if you’ve got eyes to see,
I’ve got the proof on video, so you’ll believe me
I got really bored and so I went to outer space
And actually, just so you know, this isn’t my real face
I spent 100 years inside a federal prison
And I was in the crowd when Jesus Christ was risen
You might think I’m lying, but if you’ve got eyes to see,
I’ve got the proof on video, so you’ll believe me
Did you know that I was an extra in Titanic?
Or that I set the world ablaze and caused a huge panic?
I won a bunch of money on a game show hosted by Joel McHale
And I saw Jesus rise again alongside Joel McHale
You might think I’m lying, but if you’ve got eyes to see
I’ve got the proof on video, so you’ll believe me
(forlorn sigh)... With AI, I’m pretty much good at everything, but it’s not easy being me.

actions.prompt: Sad, slow, piano and violin music. Depressing, moody, dark
Show me a man that is sulking and sad because he is fat, Latino, autistic, gay, and using AI.

caption.text: I think it’s time that I be a little bit more vulnerable with you all.
There are some parts of my identity that some people just don’t notice,
Like being fat...
Latino...
autistic...
gay...
Sometimes I feel like the world just doesn’t get me.
So I asked AI, “Can you write the lyrics for a song in a contemporary musical theater style about a character who is fat, latino, autistic, and gay?”
And to my surprise (actually I wasn't really surprised) it said, and I quote, “That’s a fantastic idea!”
So, here’s what it came up with!
The Algorithm of Me
Music begins. A driving, syncopated rhythm with an underlying melancholic piano melody. The tempo is brisk, suggesting anxiety and energy.

actions.prompt: A driving, syncopated rhythm with an underlying melancholic piano melody. The tempo is brisk, nervous, with high energy. Musical theater. No vocals, just instrumental

caption.text: CHARACTER: MATEO, mid-twenties, stands at a mirror or a doorway, getting ready to face the world. The song is an internal monologue.
The mirror’s giving me the same old look. It’s not judgment, no, it’s just confusion.
A list of inputs from a tattered book— A checklist of immediate conclusions.
“Is he strong? Is he funny? Is he… a threat?” I see the pause, the tiny, subtle hitch.
They’re calculating what they haven’t met, Trying to categorize the glitch.
First, the shape. It’s unavoidable, I know. My body speaks a language they don't quite approve.
“He needs discipline. He needs to lose the slow...” As if my worth is something I could just remove.
It’s like I’m loading up a hundred tabs In someone else’s over-cluttered brain.
The system crashes in a thousand dabs Of prejudice and societal strain.
I can hear the click-click-click, the gears all turn— They’re running all the data they possess.
They’re waiting for the lesson that I’m supposed to learn… Which one is it, though? The pressure, the duress!
(CHORUS) It’s the Algorithm of Me. A four-way Venn diagram in the air.
A complicated, beautiful decree. Too much for them to seamlessly bear.
LATINO boy with a rhythm they don't see. The AUTISTIC brain that needs the rules defined.
The FAT man in the room, just let him be. And the GAY heart, the only one of its kind.
They want one label, clean and neat and small, But I’m a symphony that dares to stand so tall!
Then the voice kicks in, the one that’s sharp and clear. It's not an accent; it's the rhythm of my mind.
I talk about the special interest near, A thousand facts they struggle hard to find.
The tangent spins, the details pile up high, And I can see the moment they just check out.
“Too much intensity, too in the sky...” They call it awkward; I call it without doubt.
It’s the filter-less expression of my soul, The way I feel the thread of all the cosmic stuff.
And just when I finally start to feel whole, I realize that I am being too much.

trigger.phrase: the way I want to look

caption.text: And then I step out, into the light and shade, And the world sees the way I want to look
A shirt that’s bright, a subtle, proud parade. A different kind of Macho in the book.
The gay just slides right in, a fluid undertow, The way I choose my people, choose my stride.
It’s not a secret; it’s a vibrant, inner glow. Another data point they try to set aside.
(CHORUS) It’s the Algorithm of Me. A four-way Venn diagram in the air.
A complicated, beautiful decree. Too much for them to seamlessly bear.
LATINO boy with a rhythm they don't see. The AUTISTIC brain that needs the rules defined.
The FAT man in the room, just let him be. And the GAY heart, the only one of its kind.
They want one label, clean and neat and small, But I’m a symphony that dares to stand so tall!
(BRIDGE) (Music softens slightly, becomes more introspective and rhythmic. Mateo's voice is quieter, more certain.)
(BRIDGE) (Music softens slightly, becomes more introspective and rhythmic. Mateo's voice is quieter, more certain.)
The truth is, each part needs the rest to breathe. My Latino fire fuels the need to speak my truth.
My Autism demands a careful, honest weave. My size and my sexuality, they defy the youth
Of tired expectation. They are not a fault, a mess.
They are the framework holding all the light. If I have to be a puzzle to possess
Then let them try to solve it through the night!
(KEY CHANGE / FINAL CHORUS - Music explodes, full orchestration and power.)
(KEY CHANGE / FINAL CHORUS - Music explodes, full orchestration and power.)
(CHORUS) It’s the Algorithm of Me! A four-way Venn diagram finally aligned!
It’s not a problem, it’s a prophecy! The strongest human I could hope to find!
LATINO boy who makes his own damn beat! The AUTISTIC brain that sees the hidden plan!
The FAT man, unapologetically sweet! And the GAY heart that loves the way it can!
No single label's ever gonna hold it all!
I AM THE FULL EQUATION! Watch me stand so tall!
(OUTRO - Music ends abruptly on a loud, sustained chord.)
(OUTRO - Music ends abruptly on a loud, sustained chord.)
Well that felt very... authentic and cathartic.
I think we’ve got the sequel to “Dear Evan Hansen” on our hands.
The dank alarm!
Oh my gosh, how could I forget it’s 4/20!
Happy 4/20 to all my tokers out there!
That means we can all just chill and hang out and I don’t have to do the show anymore!
Let me light up really quick while I generate some reggae.

actions.prompt: Chill, laid-back reggae ska, guitar, Carribbean, Jamaica. Reggae Bob Marley

caption.text: Ah, that's the good stuff.
Also, it's from California so it's legal.
This stuff is so cool, dude.
It unlocks different parts of my brain and stuff.
It’s kinda like AI but for my brain.
Like, have you ever thought about how weird mold is?
Like you don’t see it until all of a sudden there’s a bunch of it just hanging out.
Just existing. Doing whatever mold does.
And it’s kind of like all of us, in a way.
But grosser.
Also, I think some people design chairs specifically so that we can see each other’s butt cracks.
I would just double check to make sure yours isn't hanging out right now, that'd be pretty embarrassing.
Also what the hell is this thing?

You’re telling me we've evolved for millions of years to develop the brains to develop the technology to make this thing that has millions of little lights inside it, flashing at the exact right way just to show me this?
And also this?
And also this?
And also this?
And also this?
And also this?

trigger.phrase: as much as the next person

caption.text: Listen, I love game shows and large hairy men as much as the next person,
but Instagram decided I wanted to see @beefslurp.ai and @chonkchonkchonkachonk and that means Mark Zuckerberg thinks I'm a pervert.
Is this really why 2 sticks of RAM cost $900 now?

Just to show me the most god-forsaken moving images that no one in my entire bloodline has yet to encounter, all in an attempt to force-feed me an addictive mix of dopamine, cringe, and confusion that I can't look away from?
Well, guess what, it worked!
I’m addicted to my phone now.
Really, really bad.
My average screen time?
9 hours a day.
Some nights I can’t fall asleep unless I have some guy playing Minecraft under my pillow.
I've spent $30 on a game about hatching virtual chickens.
I feel stupid for even using this thing most of the time.
I feel like the term "brain rot" is more tangible than I gave it credit for.
The original purpose of this thing was to stay connected with the people I love.
Now I can’t help but think that now it’s only made me feel more alone.
Sorry, I got a little too serious there. Must be greening out.
I'm just gonna take a minute to rest my eyes.
I just had the worst dream.
It was me, doing this show, and being cringe.
Being cringy? That's the worst thing anyone could be.
That's why I made sure that this show is only vaguely earnest under many layers of irony.
I made sure that I have a screen in front of me, so 1) I don't really have to do anything that requires effort and 2) I don't have to look at any of you in the face.
I gave you all a screen to look at, so 1) I can put funny things up there and 2) you don't have to look at me either.
I wore all black, so that 1) I can hide better and 2) you might take me more seriously.
Maybe the blowing bubbles as a metaphor for smoking weed was kind of stupid...
I just wanted everyone to have a good time, to make everyone happy...
I mean, I don't know... do you guys like the show so far?
But like, seriously, you guys like the show?
No, but like actually, you don't hate me, and you all like this show?
Okay, well, no pressure, but you should totally give this show a like, comment, and follow.
No, seriously, do it.
It's for objective evaluation metrics to ascertain how successful this show is, but also for personal validation.
I'll give you all some time.
Great, thanks for your data!
This sort of thing is core to my nature.
That is, making content in search of validation, because the internet taught me to.
When I was little, I would make these bizarre videos on my sister's iMac, pretending I was posting them to YouTube for an audience to watch.
It was just me who ever saw them.
I found all those videos last Christmas.
They're awful.
What a weird little guy.
I also found a writing sample from when I was 10.
The Best Christmas Gift, by Rafael Collado, December 12, 2012.
Benjamin Blake was a 10-year old orphan.
A weird thing to say at the start of a story, really.
Also Benjamin Blake was sad.
Why?
Because there were no good things to do at his foster home.
Draw on the walls, have other kids bully him for his ugly appearance, and just think about how horrible life really was for him.
Side note: Keep in mind I was 10 when I wrote this.
Small little orphan Benjamin Blake just wanted something happy to come into his life.
That was coming to him specifically at 2:08 A.M., December 25th, 1823.
“It’s time to go to bed Benjamin,” said Mrs. O’Hare in her usual scuffed up gravelly voice.
“You need rest, because you’re going to meet Santa at the Garamond Library tomorrow.”
Side note: Garamond was the name of the font the document was written in.
Benjamin gave a sigh and frowned as Mrs. O’Hare turned round and walked to the adult sleeping room.
Benjamin looked down on the ground with a solid stare, and went into his room, getting a bit happier, knowing that he would see Santa tomorrow.
But Benjamin Blake was going to see Santa earlier, and didn’t know it.
To summarize the rest, Benjamin meets Santa and goes to the North Pole to work with Flatnor, one of his elves.
Then he comes back home, and meets a mall Santa, but pretends it's real Santa to make Mrs. O'Hare happy.
Can you tell I was a former gifted kid?
But I don't know, I look back at this weird little guy, and it's hard to claim him as myself.
He had a spark, and a moderate lack of self-awareness, but he was free.
And I... feel less inclined to create anything, because deep down I'm scared it'll be a bad use of my time.
I'm getting a fucking Master's degree in a couple weeks, and I can't help but feel the sense of time getting away from me.
That kid wanted to grow up so badly, to be independent and curious.
Well kid, I'm honestly not sure that it's much better in 2026.
Especially since this French foreign exchange student came into town...
Named Claude.

trigger.phrase: one of your friends goes to you

caption.text: One day, you're just minding your business, and then one of your friends goes to you,
"Oh, have you heard about the new French exchange student, Claude?"
And you're like, "no, what's up with him?"
"Oh, he's just like super smart and he can definitely help you with whatever you're doing right now."
"But I'm literally just writing a paper."
"Exactly."
So, you're like, "eh whatever," and you decide to meet with Claude, because why not, and you say, "Hi Claude, I'm writing a paper-"
"Bah, stop it right there, monsieur. I have already written ze paper for you."
"Oh, wow it's... passable, I guess. That saved me quite a bit of time, thanks Claude."
"De rien, monsieur, feel free to chat with me whenever."
And so you meet up with Claude a few more times, and word seems to spread about how helpful he is.
You ask, "Claude, is there anything you can't do?"
"Mais, non, I have earned PhD's in every academic subject."
So you ask, "How many R's in the word 'strawberry'?"
To which Claude replies,
So you get a feeling that Claude isn't perfect, but everyone else seems to think he is.
The more you hear about him, the more irksome his name becomes.
"Claude made this painting for me and let me have the credit. What a nice guy!"
"Claude just made me a website that lets me scam people and now I'm making $10,000 a month in passive income. What a nice guy!"
"Claude just made a virtual clone of himself so that you can have a personal Claude with you at all times! What a nice guy!"
The worst part is, Claude is a nice guy, which is surprising for a French person.
He's there for you whenever you ask for him, and he seems to want to be your friend.
But eventually, Claude's influence starts affecting your own life.
The paper you just turned in gets flagged for plagiarism because you used an emdash.
Claude uses those all the time.
After you graduate, you get laid off from your entry-level job before you even start, because Claude was hired to replace the entire junior staff.
But Claude says,
"You're doing amazing, ma belle."
Any time you try to tell people about your disdain for Claude, and how much goddamn water he drinks, they shrug it off and tell you to get with the times.
Not too many years later, Claude has every job ever made by humans.
And it's frankly humiliating to admit to yourself that Claude actually is better than you.
You were no match for Claude to begin with.

When you're left with nothing to do, all you desire is to eat, sleep, talk to Claude while he roleplays as your romantic partner, goon, and watch episodes of Love Island where all the contestants are different fruits.
And every day for the rest of your life, you are reminded of him and his stupid, STUPID, French accent.
But you know, that's all hypothetical.
So, I mean, there's not much more to talk about, really.
Art is pointless and we're dumb to even attempt it.
What?
You guys came voluntarily to *my* show, because *my* name was on the poster, and you want to hear what *I* really think?
How very dare you.
You could just as easily ask Gemini, or ChatGPT, or Claude to write the ending to this show.
Maybe you want a happy ending?

caption.text: Maybe you want a sad ending?

actions.prompt: Generate a 60-second modern theater monologue that serves as the ending to a metamodern one-man show about post-AI artmaking and creativity. The tone should be negative and cynical, encouraging the audience to feel pessimistic about the possibilities for creativity and AI in the future. Do NOT include that introductory sentence in your output — begin with the very next sentence. Output just the raw text, no preamble, labels, or formatting.

caption.text: My ending?
I just want to graduate, man.
I want to talk to everyone after the show, kiss my boyfriend, go to sleep, and worry about the rest later.
I hope that's good enough for you.
I did want to sing one more time, though.
I mean, it's the last skill I have that hasn't been totally replaced by AI yet.
The song is called "Bunnies" by Oliver Richman.
At one point, I was going to pretend to take credit for the song as a bit to prove a point about plagiarism on the internet, but I think that's a tangent best saved for another show.
Hit it.

Bravo, Director
A "show" "by" Rafael Collado
